executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors, including high-profile targets like Canada Goose, Kids & Company childcare, Eastern Townships School Board, and Peterborough Public Health. The Canadian government (CCCS) issued an urgent advisory about 'FortiBleed'—thousands of compromised Fortinet VPN credentials have leaked, enabling attackers to bypass authentication on corporate networks. Qilin ransomware leads the assault with 21 victims, followed by aggressive campaigns from Play, Clop, SafePay, and TheGentlemen groups. National Money Mart (a major payday lender) suffered a database breach by Everest ransomware, and the Municipality of North Perth was hit, signaling attacks on critical public infrastructure.

items.description: Leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials enabling unauthorized access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Critical, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware siege with 103 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors. CCCS issued Alert AL26-014 warning of FortiBleed—a mass leak of Fortinet VPN credentials now being exploited to breach Canadian networks. Public sector targets include Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé, alongside critical infrastructure like Eeyou Communications Network (Indigenous telecom).
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel retailer) breached by Coinbase Cartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) breached by Sinobi; Kids & Company (major childcare chain) breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday lender) database leaked by Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; Trucash (financial services) breached by Lynx; multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy compromised

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco is patching a second actively exploited zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20262) in its SD-WAN Manager within two weeks, allowing attackers to escalate privileges to root. CISA has also flagged a LiteSpeed cPanel plugin flaw (CVE-2026-54420) being exploited for root access. The DragonForce ransomware gang is abusing Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure to hide command-and-control traffic, successfully breaching a major U.S. services firm. Multiple high-profile data breaches were disclosed including iRhythm (patient medical data), Infinite Campus (137,000 school staff accounts), Novo Nordisk (patient data and AI assets), and Kodak. China-linked threat actors are actively targeting U.S. and Canadian medical research institutions using legacy REDCap exploits.

items.description: Cisco SD-WAN vManage privilege escalation to root

canada_landscape.summary: China-linked threat actors are actively targeting Canadian medical research institutions using legacy REDCap exploits to steal sensitive research data. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. The Ford government in Ontario reportedly knew about a cyber attack on a health agency a month before publicly addressing it, according to newly released documents.
